name: ETH Value Reacceptance Exhaustion v1
edge_mechanism: mean reversion from exhaustion only when structure reaccepts prior value
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner lane for testing a constrained exhaustion reversion idea inside a mostly choppy regime. The edge is not raw fade-everything behavior; it is a selective fade only after price overextends and then reaccepts prior value.
invalidation_condition: Abort if value reacceptance after exhaustion remains too sparse, if reclaimed moves fail to mean-revert enough after costs, or if ongoing directional persistence overwhelms the fade thesis.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price overextends away from fast value, loses directional drive, reaccepts prior value, and then mean-reverts far enough for a controlled fade harvest.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy conditions still produce exhaustion overshoots. Waiting for value reacceptance should avoid fading strength or weakness too early.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is cleaner than SOL or TAO for a reacceptance fade and should reveal whether the mechanism is real rather than noise-driven.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may keep drifting one-way after reacceptance attempts, making the fade thesis too weak after fees.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If reacceptance entries are valid but moves are too small, reduce target ambition and tighten persistence-avoidance before changing the core idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective exhaustion fades with quicker time-to-target than the continuation specs and limited runner dependence.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h and at least one cross-lane show enough reacceptance-fade density with positive post-cost expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated fades steamrolled by ongoing continuation.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: managed by allowing exhaustion plus reacceptance instead of demanding extreme trend and reversal simultaneously
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: broader than ceremonial failure sequences because it uses value reacceptance rather than exact breakdown choreography

test_lanes.why_included: Cleaner lane for testing selective exhaustion reacceptance against the mixed backdrop.
test_lanes.continue_if: Reacceptance fades show usable density and controlled post-cost reversions.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Setups remain sparse or ongoing persistence overwhelms the fades.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A modest number of overshoot-and-reaccept fades that revert back toward value.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same reacceptance grammar survives on a more expressive lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: SOL preserves the setup without degenerating into chaos.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: SOL becomes too noisy for controlled fades.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Bigger but less orderly exhaustion fades than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives outside the major-asset lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO still produces recognizable reacceptance-fade behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The pattern becomes too sparse or too violent.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but sharp reversions after obvious overshoots.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_20 + 0.22 * ATR_14 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close through EMA_20 - 0.22 * ATR_14 or 4 bars
